findings.detail: Latest catalog ATLAS heartbeat docs/catalog/heartbeats/2026-05-16T11-06-09Z-atlas.json: cycle C-313, gi 0.71, mode yellow, source kv, twelve elevated anomalies, kv_keys present for core GI/signal/vault keys, seed_result 200 OK, sustain_eligible false.
GET /api/integrity-status: source kv (not mock); gi_provenance kv-live; degraded true; global_integrity 0.75; mode yellow; gi_verified false; cycle C-313.

GET /api/signals/micro: composite gi 0.675, errors 9, payload cycle C-306 vs integrity-status cycle C-313 — treat micro cycle label as possibly stale. ATLAS-named elevated lanes largely still elevated in live sweep; CrossRef and NASA APIs read nominal now versus ATLAS heartbeat list (severity improved / drift).

GI band spans catalog heartbeat (0.71), live micro gi (0.675), and KV integrity-status global_integrity (0.75); integrity marks gi_verified false — consistent yellow stress but not a single reconciled headline scalar.
GET /api/epicon/candidates returned count 0 — no candidates, verification skipped (no POST /api/zeus/verify or /api/epicon/publish).
POST /api/vault/attest returned HTTP 404; quorum_status not available — C-298 route not present on deployed surface.
POST /api/agents/journal returned HTTP 502 with error github_403; mirrored_to_kv false — substrate mirror blocked this pass.

recommendation: Treat headline GI as KV-backed live (not mock) but compare to fresh micro composite before operator decisions. Restore or deploy POST /api/vault/attest for ZEUS quorum registration. Fix GitHub substrate credentials for agent journal writes. Monitor persistent upstream failures (OpenAQ, NWS, data.gov, UN, OECD, npm latency, self-ping, Cloudflare, Fastly, CT logs, FRED).
